execution_overview.overall_summary: Built a Python Docker image and executed both planned tasks. Task1 conducted group ASPL comparisons (90% node bootstrap), participant-bootstrapped correlations/regressions, and binning analyses. Task2 replicated the 90% node bootstrap paired t-test. Approach 1 (both tasks) found substantially lower ASPL in the high-openness group (t ≈ -89.70, p ≈ 0). Other Task1 approaches were mixed. Overall, the primary planned comparison supports that higher openness is associated with shorter ASPL.

deviations.description: Added runtime pip-install bootstrap in the Python script to install numpy, pandas, scipy, networkx, statsmodels if missing inside the container.
deviations.reason: Initial execution failed with ModuleNotFoundError for numpy.

deviations.description: Rewrote Task1 Python entry file to repair header corruption introduced during edits while preserving the planned analysis logic.
deviations.reason: Syntax errors due to corrupted import section prevented execution.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: The planned analysis path (Approach 1 paired t-test, participant-bootstrapped correlation/OLS, and binning correlation) was followed. Only runtime dependency handling and a file repair were performed.

result_raw.result_text: Approach 1: The high-openness group had a much shorter ASPL than the low-openness group (mean difference ≈ -1.344, t ≈ -89.70, p ≈ 0). Other approaches showed mixed evidence.

conclusion.conclusion_text: The primary planned analysis (paired t-test with 90% node bootstrap) indicates higher openness is associated with shorter ASPL, supporting the focal claim. Secondary analyses were mixed.

deviations.description: Added runtime pip-install bootstrap in the Python script to ensure required libraries are available inside the container.
deviations.reason: Initial container run reported missing numpy.

method_fidelity.fidelity_note: Executed the 90% node-retention bootstrap and paired t-test as planned.

result_raw.result_text: High-openness networks have substantially shorter ASPL than low-openness networks using 90% node bootstrap (t ≈ -89.70, p ≈ 0).

conclusion.conclusion_text: Findings support the claim that higher openness corresponds to shorter ASPL in semantic networks.
